title: Educational Lab Platform Architecture Work
summary: Private educational lab platform work focused on onboarding, stack comprehension, architecture clarity, documentation alignment, and security-conscious review of cloud deployment flows.

logs.title: Merged 10-day onboarding architecture package
logs.details: Added a structured onboarding package that maps the platform across routing, stack responsibilities, frontend-to-backend flow, authentication, lab listing, launch flow, lab execution, backend session lifecycle, remote machine architecture, integrations, cloud infrastructure, infrastructure as code, and production architecture.

logs.title: Opened documentation cleanup and accuracy audit
logs.details: Started a documentation-only cleanup pass to inventory the docs structure, compare documentation claims against the current codebase, update GitHub-readable indexes, clarify local setup around the main application, and separate current implementation reality from older architecture notes.

logs.title: Advanced documentation audit and lab session architecture mapping
logs.details: Reviewed top-level and main technical documentation, converted a generic dependency note into repo-specific contributor guidance, added a current lab session flow diagram, and marked older visual architecture material as partially accurate instead of treating it as source of truth.

logs.title: Paused cloud quick start rewrite pending maintainer direction
logs.details: Identified that the cloud quick start should wait for the related deployment flow review to settle before being rewritten. The next pass is intentionally approval-gated so the documentation reflects the accepted path rather than stale setup assumptions.

logs.title: Reviewed and hardened cloud lab lifecycle security flow
logs.details: Completed a focused security review and cleanup pass around the cloud lab lifecycle boundary. Work included improving token-validation behavior, enforcing authentication before infrastructure side effects, reducing authorization-role blast radius, removing exposure of an incomplete image-capture route, making approved image configuration fail closed, adjusting short authorization caching so it works safely across protected lifecycle routes, removing raw request logging from lifecycle automation, documenting a migration caveat for older unmanaged instances, and opening follow-up issues for larger architecture decisions. Final automated review returned with no major issues; live cloud validation remains pending.

logs.title: Mapped creator-mode lab versioning across virtual-machine and container-backed labs
logs.details: Reviewed a proposed creator-mode lab versioning model against cloud container and virtual-machine behavior. The current working abstraction is to open an editable lab environment, modify it, save a draft version, test it, and publish after validation. Virtual-machine-backed labs map naturally to managed creator environments that can be saved into new machine-image-backed lab version records. Container-backed labs should use a reproducible artifact path: edit source or workspace files, build a new immutable image, push it to a container registry with a versioned identifier, register a new runtime definition, then record and test that version before publishing. This clarified that live running-container capture is likely not the clean source-of-truth model; the safer implementation is to treat container images as built artifacts rather than mutable running environments. The conclusion is still intentionally bounded: the abstraction looks sound, while the exact container creator workflow needs implementation design, cost review, and live cloud validation.

nextSteps: Wait for maintainer review and live cloud validation of the current security pull request
Validate the updated lab lifecycle deployment path once limited cloud access or maintainer-run smoke tests are available
Rewrite the cloud quick start only after the updated deployment and security path is accepted as the source of truth
Continue documentation audit with non-blocked technical docs

Track larger hardening follow-ups separately: stronger role isolation, proper machine-image capture workflow design, final authorization cache strategy, and container-backed lab versioning workflow design
Validate the practical MVP for container-backed labs: pull from a container registry, edit/build locally or in a controlled workspace, push a versioned image, and update the runtime definition
Convert approved architecture findings into recruiter/client-readable project notes
blockers: Live environment validation requires limited cloud access or maintainer-provided verification
Cloud quick start rewrite depends on maintainer approval of the updated deployment path
Older visual diagrams should be retained until maintainers confirm whether to replace, rebuild, or archive them
Container-backed lab versioning still needs a confirmed implementation path before it should be documented as final behavior
